title: 2022年度 第2回 英検準1級 リーディング

vocabulary.example: Let the frozen chicken thaw before cooking.
Parents try to instill good values in their children.
She made an earnest effort to improve her grades.

He was ridiculed for his unusual fashion choices.
The bashful child hid behind her mother.
The rotary phone is a relic from a bygone era.
The dog growled at the stranger approaching the gate.

The city is notorious for its traffic jams.
Years of practice finally paid off when she won the gold medal.
It took a while, but she finally caught on to the joke.

Orion is one of the most recognizable constellations.
Bees are essential for the pollination of many crops.
Marathon runners need great stamina.
Her resourcefulness helped the team solve the crisis.
His unconventional approach to teaching inspired many students.
Military intelligence is crucial for successful operations.
The rocky terrain made hiking difficult.
Her persistence eventually led to success.
Motorized vehicles were banned from the nature reserve.
The battle resulted in heavy casualties on both sides.

vocabulary.meaning: 計り知れない価値のある
vocabulary.example: Your advice has been invaluable to the project.
The organization distributed pamphlets about health.
Cycling is a popular form of exercise.
The campaign gained momentum after the successful rally.
Durians are banned from many hotels due to their strong smell.
Farmers cultivate rice in paddy fields.
Organic fertilizer is better for the environment.
A grove of olive trees surrounded the farmhouse.
The apple orchard produces thousands of apples each year.
The soldiers ran low on ammunition during the battle.
Supplies were stored at the military depot.
She navigated the boat through the narrow channel.
Police officers patrol the streets at night.
The military campaign lasted six months.
The enemy's defenses were too strong to break through.
She is a versatile musician who plays five instruments.
The chef prepared an elaborate five-course meal.
The region has abundant natural resources.
Young children are vulnerable to infections.
The court found his claim to be legitimate.
Subsequent investigations revealed more evidence.
Dust tends to accumulate on unused shelves.
He was reluctant to accept the offer.
The storm caused major disruption to train services.
Justice will prevail in the end.
I reckon the meeting will last about an hour.
Don't stray too far from the campsite.
The ball shattered the window into pieces.
The cumulative effect of the changes was significant.
The pious monk spent hours in prayer each day.
Young people tend to gravitate toward big cities.

His muddled explanation confused everyone.
The imposition of new taxes angered businesses.

Full disclosure of financial information is required.

Early detection of the disease can save lives.
The company made a strategic decision to expand overseas.
The commander ordered all troops to advance.
This year's wheat harvest was excellent.
The sensor has high sensitivity to temperature changes.
Children are often more resilient than adults think.

sections.name: 大問1
sections.instruction: 次の(1)から(25)までの(　)に入れるのに最も適切なものを1，2，3，4の中から一つ選びなさい。

questions.text:
A: Mom, can you make hamburgers for dinner tonight?
B: Yes, but I'll have to take the meat out of the freezer and let it ( 1 ) first.
questions.translation:
A：ママ、今夜ハンバーガー作ってくれる？
B：いいけど、冷凍庫から肉を出して先に( 1 )させないと。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ thaw＝解凍する。冷凍肉を使う前の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 thaw＝解凍する。defrost（類義語）。thaw out＝完全に解凍する。

questions.text: Jocelyn always reminded her son not to tell lies. She believed it was important to ( 2 ) a strong sense of honesty in him.
questions.translation: ジョセリンは息子に嘘をつかないよう常に言い聞かせた。強い誠実さを( 2 )ることが大切だと信じていた。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ instill＝植え付ける。価値観を教え込む文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 instill＝植え付ける。instill A in B＝BにAを植え付ける。

questions.text: Zara was very angry with her boyfriend, but she forgave him after hearing his ( 3 ) apology. She was sure that he really was sorry.
questions.translation: ザラは彼氏にとても怒っていたが、( 3 )な謝罪を聞いて許した。本当に申し訳ないと思っていると確信した。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ earnest＝真剣な。心からの謝罪の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 earnest＝真剣な。in earnest＝本気で。

questions.text: At first, the Smiths enjoyed their backyard swimming pool, but keeping it clean became such a ( 4 ) that they left it covered most of the time.
questions.translation: 最初スミス家は裏庭のプールを楽しんでいたが、清潔に保つのが( 4 )になり、ほとんどカバーをしたままにした。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ nuisance＝迷惑。面倒になった文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 nuisance＝迷惑、厄介なもの。what a nuisance!＝なんて面倒な！

questions.text: Throughout the course of history, many great thinkers were at first ( 5 ) for their ideas before eventually being taken seriously.
questions.translation: 歴史を通じて、多くの偉大な思想家は最終的に真剣に受け止められる前に、最初はアイデアのために( 5 )された。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ ridiculed＝嘲笑された。後に認められた文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 ridicule＝嘲笑する。ridiculous（形容詞）＝ばかげた。

questions.text: At first, the little girl felt ( 6 ) in front of the large audience at the speech contest, but after about a minute she began to feel more confident.
questions.translation: 最初、その少女はスピーチコンテストの大勢の聴衆の前で( 6 )と感じたが、1分ほどで自信を持ち始めた。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ bashful＝恥ずかしがりの。大勢の前での緊張の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 bashful＝恥ずかしがりの。shy, timid（類義語）。

questions.text: Typewriters are a ( 7 ) of the past. They remind us how far technology has advanced since they were common in offices and homes.
questions.translation: タイプライターは過去の( 7 )だ。オフィスや家庭で一般的だった頃から技術がどれだけ進歩したか思い出させる。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ relic＝遺物。過去の技術の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 relic＝遺物。a relic of the past＝過去の遺物。

questions.text: When the man approached the tiger's cage, the huge animal ( 8 ) deeply. The man stepped back in fear at the terrifying sound.
questions.translation: 男がトラの檻に近づくと、巨大な動物が深く( 8 )した。男は恐ろしい音に恐怖で後ずさりした。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ growled＝唸った。トラの恐ろしい音の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 growl＝唸る、うなり声を上げる。

questions.text: Police officers must promise to ( 9 ) the law. This includes, of course, following the law themselves.
questions.translation: 警察官は法律を( 9 )ることを約束しなければならない。もちろん自分自身も法律に従うことを含む。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ uphold＝擁護する。法律を守る文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 uphold＝擁護する、支持する。uphold the law＝法律を守る。

questions.text: All employees have a ( 10 ) medical checkup every year. Companies are required by law to make sure all their workers do it.
questions.translation: 全従業員は毎年( 10 )な健康診断を受ける。法律で企業はすべての労働者にこれを受けさせることが義務付けられている。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ compulsory＝義務的な。法的義務の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 compulsory＝義務的な。mandatory, obligatory（類義語）。

questions.text: Biology students must learn how cell ( 11 ) works, as this process of a single cell splitting into two is commonly found in nature.
questions.translation: 生物の学生は細胞( 11 )の仕組みを学ばなければならない。一つの細胞が二つに分裂するこのプロセスは自然界でよく見られる。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ division＝分裂。細胞分裂の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 cell division＝細胞分裂。divide（動詞）＝分割する。

questions.text: After the two companies ( 12 ), several senior employees became unnecessary and lost their jobs.
questions.translation: 2社が( 12 )した後、数名のシニア社員が不要になり職を失った。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ merged＝合併した。会社統合の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 merge＝合併する。merger（名詞）＝合併。

questions.text: In order to avoid becoming ( 13 ) while exercising, one should always drink enough water. The longer the workout, the more water is necessary.
questions.translation: 運動中に( 13 )にならないよう、十分な水を飲むべきだ。運動時間が長いほど、より多くの水が必要。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ dehydrated＝脱水状態の。運動中の水分補給の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 dehydrated＝脱水状態の。dehydration（名詞）。hydrate＝水分補給する。

questions.text: Ken was always well behaved at home, so his mother was shocked when his teacher said he was one of the most ( 14 ) students in his class.
questions.translation: ケンは家では行儀が良かったので、先生がクラスで最も( 14 )な生徒の一人だと言った時、母親は驚いた。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ disobedient＝反抗的な。家と学校の行動の差の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 disobedient＝反抗的な。obedient（対義語）＝従順な。

questions.text: The police questioned ( 15 ) at the scene of the crime, hoping someone who had been nearby had seen what happened.
questions.translation: 警察は犯罪現場にいた( 15 )に事情聴取した。近くにいた誰かが何が起きたか見ていることを期待して。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ bystanders＝傍観者。犯罪現場の目撃者の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 bystander＝傍観者。innocent bystanders＝無関係の傍観者。

questions.text: Several generals attempted to ( 16 ) the country's prime minister. However, they were unsuccessful, and he remains in power.
questions.translation: 数名の将軍が首相を( 16 )しようとした。しかし失敗し、彼は権力を維持している。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ overthrow＝打倒する。クーデター未遂の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 overthrow＝打倒する。coup d'état（類義語のフランス語）。

questions.text: Caleb finished a draft of his proposal, so he asked his manager to ( 17 ) it. Unfortunately, she thought it still needed a lot of improvement.
questions.translation: カレブは提案書の草稿を書き終えたので、上司に( 17 )してもらうよう頼んだ。残念ながら彼女はまだ多くの改善が必要だと思った。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ evaluate＝評価する。草稿のレビューの文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 evaluate＝評価する。evaluation（名詞）＝評価。

questions.text: American presidents Thomas Jefferson and John Adams exchanged letters with each other for over 50 years. This ( 18 ) is an important part of American history.
questions.translation: アメリカ大統領のジェファソンとアダムスは50年以上手紙を交わした。この( 18 )はアメリカ史の重要な一部だ。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ correspondence＝書簡。手紙のやり取りの文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 correspondence＝書簡。correspond（動詞）＝文通する。

questions.text: During the riot, the town was in a state of ( 19 ). People were out in the streets fighting and breaking windows, and many stores were robbed.
questions.translation: 暴動中、町は( 19 )状態にあった。人々は通りで戦い窓を壊し、多くの店が略奪された。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ anarchy＝無政府状態。暴動の混乱の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 anarchy＝無政府状態。anarchist（名詞）＝アナーキスト。

questions.text: The flowers of some plants are actually ( 20 ) and can be used to make salads both more delicious and more visually attractive.
questions.translation: 一部の植物の花は実は( 20 )で、サラダをより美味しく見た目も魅力的にするのに使える。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ edible＝食べられる。サラダに使う文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 edible＝食べられる。inedible（対義語）。edible flowers＝食用花。

questions.text: No one was surprised when the famous scientist made many mistakes during his speech. He is ( 21 ) for his poor speaking skills.
questions.translation: 有名な科学者がスピーチ中に多くの間違いをしても誰も驚かなかった。彼はスピーチの下手さで( 21 )だ。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ notorious＝悪名高い。悪い評判の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 notorious＝悪名高い。notorious for＝～で悪名高い。

questions.text: All of Brad's hard work and long hours ( 22 ) when his boss gave him a promotion last month.
questions.translation: ブラッドの努力と長時間労働が先月上司が昇進をくれた時に( 22 )した。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ paid off＝報われた。昇進の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 pay off＝報われる。All the hard work paid off.

questions.text: Since the CEO's speech was so vague, it took Gina a while to ( 23 ) to the fact that the company was in serious financial trouble.
questions.translation: CEOのスピーチが曖昧だったので、会社が深刻な財務問題にあるという事実にジーナが( 23 )するのに時間がかかった。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ catch on＝気づく。曖昧な情報から理解する文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 catch on＝気づく、理解する。catch on to＝～に気づく。

questions.text: Each member of the team has a job to do for the new project, but the responsibility for coordinating all of their efforts ( 24 ) the manager.
questions.translation: チームの各メンバーは新プロジェクトで仕事があるが、全員の努力を調整する責任はマネージャーに( 24 )。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ falls on＝～にかかる。責任の所在の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 fall on＝（責任が）～にかかる。The blame falls on him.

questions.text: The employee tried to ( 25 ) his theft from the company by destroying files and other evidence that proved his guilt.
questions.translation: その従業員はファイルや有罪を証明する証拠を破壊して会社からの窃盗を( 25 )しようとした。
choiceAnalysis: ✅ cover up＝隠蔽する。犯罪隠蔽の文脈に合致
questions.grammar: 💡 cover up＝隠蔽する。cover-up（名詞）＝隠蔽工作。

sections.name: 大問2
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，Bを読み，その文意にそって(26)から(31)までの(　)に入れるのに最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: Nabta Playa's Stone Circle
paragraphs: Many prehistoric societies constructed stone circles. These were created for various reasons, such as tracking the sun's movement. The oldest such circle known to scientists can be found at Nabta Playa in Egypt. At around 7,000 years old, this circle predates England's Stonehenge — probably the world's best-known prehistoric stone circle — by more than 1,000 years. Nabta Playa's climate is extremely dry today, but this was not always the case. ( 26 ), heavy seasonal rainfall during the period when the circle was built led to the formation of temporary lakes, and these attracted cattle-grazing tribes to the area.

Nabta Playa's first settlers arrived around 10,000 years ago. Archaeologists have uncovered evidence that these settlers created a system of deep wells that gave them access to water year-round, and that they arranged their homes in straight rows and equipped them with storage spaces. They also practiced a religion that focused on the worship of cattle, which were central to their lives. These discoveries are evidence that the settlers ( 27 ).

Research findings show that some of the circle's stones would have lined up with the sun on the longest day of the year around 7,000 years ago. This suggests the circle was used as a calendar. One astrophysicist, however, believes the circle ( 28 ). He points out that the positions of other stones match those of stars in the constellation Orion at the time the circle was built. Because of this, he proposes that the circle was an astrological map showing the positions of stars in the night sky.
translations: 多くの先史社会がストーンサークルを建造した。太陽の動きの追跡など様々な理由で作られた。科学者に知られる最古のサークルはエジプトのナブタ・プラヤにある。約7,000年前のもので、世界で最も有名なストーンヘンジより1,000年以上古い。ナブタ・プラヤの気候は現在非常に乾燥しているが、常にそうだったわけではない。( 26 )、サークルが建設された時期の大量の季節的降雨が一時的な湖を形成し、牧畜部族を引きつけた。
ナブタ・プラヤの最初の定住者は約1万年前に到着した。考古学者は定住者が年間を通じて水にアクセスできる深い井戸システムを作り、家を一直線に配置し収納スペースを備えていたことを発見した。彼らはまた牛の崇拝を中心とした宗教を実践していた。これらの発見は定住者が( 27 )ことの証拠だ。
研究結果はサークルの石の一部が約7,000年前の夏至の日に太陽と一直線に並んだことを示している。これはカレンダーとして使われたことを示唆する。しかしある天体物理学者はサークルが( 28 )と考えている。他の石の位置がサークル建設時のオリオン座の星の位置と一致すると指摘し、夜空の星の位置を示す天文学的地図だったと提案している。
sentencePairs: the oldest such circle can be found at Nabta Playa
最古のサークルはナブタ・プラヤにある
heavy seasonal rainfall led to the formation of temporary lakes
大量の季節的降雨が一時的な湖を形成した
they arranged their homes in straight rows
家を一直線に配置した
the circle was used as a calendar
カレンダーとして使われた
the circle was an astrological map
天文学的地図だった

choiceTranslations: 一方で
実は
これにもかかわらず
同様に
choiceAnalysis: 乾燥→しかし常にそうでない→実は降雨があった（補足説明）→正解。💡 補足説明は前文の情報を詳しく掘り下げる
逆接ではない（前文の補足）
sourceEvidence: Nabta Playa's climate is extremely dry today, but this was not always the case

choiceTranslations: 宗教的な考えに疑問を持った
牧畜への関心を失った
深刻な内部紛争を経験した
洗練された社会を発展させた
choiceAnalysis: 宗教への疑問は述べていない
内部紛争は述べていない
井戸システム、整然とした住居、収納、宗教→洗練された社会→正解。💡 因果関係の問いでは、原因→結果の論理チェーンを正確に追跡する
sourceEvidence: created a system of deep wells
arranged their homes in straight rows
equipped them with storage spaces

choices: caused people to avoid the area
choiceTranslations: 別の目的もあった
もっと早く作られた
もともと別の場所に建てられた
人々を避けさせた
choiceAnalysis: カレンダーだけでなく天文学的地図としても使われた（別の目的）→正解。💡 空所前後の文脈から論理的に合う選択肢を判断する
人々を避けさせたは述べていない
sourceEvidence: This suggests the circle was used as a calendar
he proposes that the circle was an astrological map

passages.label: B
passages.title: The Good Roads Movement
paragraphs: Beginning in the late nineteenth century, the Good Roads Movement transformed America's landscape, helping to create the nation's system of roads and highways. This movement ( 29 ). While most people today assume that the road system was first developed in response to the needs of automobile drivers, this is a myth. Actually, the demand started mainly with cyclists. The invention of the modern bicycle led to a cycling craze in the 1890s, and millions of Americans wanted better, safer roads to cycle on.

Cyclists began pressuring local governments to improve the quality of roads, which were often poorly maintained and dangerous. At first, the movement was resisted by farmers, who did not want their tax dollars to be spent supporting the leisure activities of cyclists from cities. Gradually, however, farmers ( 30 ). One reason for this was an influential pamphlet called The Gospel of Good Roads: A Letter to the American Farmer. It convinced many farmers by emphasizing the benefits of roads, such as making it easier for them to transport their crops to markets.

As automobiles became common, the movement quickly gained momentum. ( 31 ), the invention of the Ford Model T in the early 1900s led to many new drivers, who were also eager for better roads. Millions of these affordable cars were sold, and the increase in drivers put pressure on governments to build more roads and improve the quality of existing ones.
translations: 19世紀後半に始まった良道路運動はアメリカの景観を変え、国の道路・高速道路システムの構築を助けた。この運動は( 29 )。今日ほとんどの人は道路システムが自動車ドライバーのニーズに応えて開発されたと思っているが、それは神話だ。実際には需要は主に自転車乗りから始まった。近代的な自転車の発明は1890年代に自転車ブームを引き起こし、何百万人ものアメリカ人がより良い安全な道路を求めた。
自転車乗りは道路の質の改善を地方自治体に圧力をかけ始めた。最初、農民はこの運動に抵抗した。都市からの自転車乗りのレジャー活動を支援するために税金が使われるのを望まなかった。しかし次第に農民は( 30 )。その理由の一つは『良道路の福音：アメリカの農民への手紙』という影響力のあるパンフレットだった。農産物を市場に運びやすくなるなど道路の利点を強調して多くの農民を説得した。
自動車が普及するにつれ運動は急速に勢いを増した。( 31 )、1900年代初頭のフォード・モデルTの発明が多くの新しいドライバーを生み、彼らもより良い道路を熱望した。数百万台の手頃な車が売れ、ドライバーの増加が政府に道路建設と既存道路の改善への圧力をかけた。
sentencePairs: the demand started mainly with cyclists
需要は主に自転車乗りから始まった
farmers did not want their tax dollars to be spent
農民は税金が使われるのを望まなかった
an influential pamphlet convinced many farmers
影響力のあるパンフレットが多くの農民を説得した
the Ford Model T led to many new drivers
フォード・モデルTが多くの新しいドライバーを生んだ

choiceTranslations: 自動車メーカーによって始められた
意外な起源を持っていた
ドライバー間の不一致を生んだ
多くの自転車乗りを怒らせた
choiceAnalysis: 自動車メーカーではない
自動車ではなく自転車乗りが起源というのは意外→正解。💡 空所前後の文脈から論理的に合う選択肢を判断する
ドライバー間の不一致は述べていない
自転車乗りを怒らせたは逆
sourceEvidence: While most people today assume that the road system was first developed in response to the needs of automobile drivers, this is a myth

choiceTranslations: 抗議を増やした
別の道路を使い始めた
意見を変え始めた
自転車乗りに反対した
choiceAnalysis: 別の道路は述べていない
最初は抵抗→しかし次第に意見を変えた→正解。💡 因果関係の問いでは、原因→結果の論理チェーンを正確に追跡する
反対を続けたではない
sourceEvidence: At first, the movement was resisted by farmers
Gradually, however, farmers

choiceTranslations: 対照的に
特に
それにもかかわらず
したがって
choiceAnalysis: 自動車の普及の具体例としてフォード・モデルTを挙げている→正解。💡 空所前後の文脈から論理的に合う選択肢を判断する
sourceEvidence: As automobiles became common, the movement quickly gained momentum
the invention of the Ford Model T

sections.name: 大問3
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，B，Cの内容に関して，質問に対して最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: Recognizing Faces
paragraphs: Humans are generally very good at recognizing faces and quickly interpreting their expressions. This is achieved by having specific areas of the brain that specialize in processing facial features. The development of this ability makes sense in terms of evolution, since early humans would have needed to judge, for example, whether those around them were angry and therefore potentially dangerous. One unintended consequence, however, is that people often think they see faces on objects in their environment. People perceive these so-called false faces on a variety of objects, from clouds and tree trunks to pieces of food and electric sockets.

Researchers in Australia recently performed a study to learn more about how the brain processes false faces. Previous studies have revealed that for real faces, people's judgment of what emotion a face is expressing is affected by the faces they have just seen. Seeing a series of happy faces, for example, tends to make people assess the face they next see as expressing happiness. In the Australian study, the researchers showed participants a series of false faces that expressed a particular emotion. They found that, as with real faces, the participants' judgments of the emotions expressed by the false faces were affected by the ones they had just been shown. Based on this finding, the researchers concluded that the brain processes false faces in a way similar to how it processes real ones.

The researchers also noted that any object with features that even loosely resemble the layout of a human face — two eyes and a nose above a mouth — can trigger the brain to assess those features for emotional expression. In other words, the brain's criteria for recognizing a face are general rather than specific. The researchers say this is one reason the brain can assess facial expressions so quickly.
translations: 人間は一般的に顔の認識と表情の素早い解釈が得意だ。これは顔の特徴の処理に特化した脳の特定領域によって達成される。この能力の発達は進化の観点から理にかなっている。初期の人間は周囲の人が怒っているかどうか（つまり危険かどうか）を判断する必要があったからだ。しかし意図しない結果として、環境中の物体に顔を見ることがある。雲や木の幹、食べ物や電気コンセントなど様々な物体にこれらの「偽の顔」を知覚する。
オーストラリアの研究者は脳が偽の顔をどう処理するか調べた。先行研究で実際の顔について、感情の判断が直前に見た顔に影響されることが明らかになっていた。例えば幸せな顔の連続を見ると次の顔も幸せだと評価しやすい。オーストラリアの研究では特定の感情を表す偽の顔の連続を見せた結果、実際の顔と同様に判断が影響されることが判明した。研究者は脳が偽の顔を実際の顔と同様に処理すると結論づけた。
研究者はまた、人間の顔の配置（口の上に二つの目と鼻）にわずかでも似ている物体が脳に感情表情の評価を引き起こすことを指摘した。つまり脳の顔認識基準は具体的ではなく一般的だ。これが顔の表情を素早く評価できる理由の一つだという。
sentencePairs: specific areas of the brain specialize in processing facial features
脳の特定領域が顔の特徴の処理に特化
people often think they see faces on objects
環境中の物体に顔を見ることがある
the brain processes false faces in a way similar to real ones
脳は偽の顔を実際の顔と同様に処理する
the brain's criteria for recognizing a face are general rather than specific
顔認識基準は具体的ではなく一般的

questions.question: In the first paragraph, why does the author of the passage mention objects such as clouds?
questions.questionTranslation: 第1段落で著者はなぜ雲などの物体に言及しているか？
choices: To support the idea that people's surroundings can affect how well they are able to judge the emotions of others.
To describe how people who cannot identify faces also have trouble identifying certain other objects.
To help explain that our reactions to everyday objects in our environment are controlled by different areas of the brain.
To provide examples of everyday things on which people imagine they can see faces.
choiceTranslations: 周囲の環境が他者の感情の判断能力に影響するという考えを支持するため。
顔を識別できない人は他の物体の識別にも問題を抱えていると説明するため。
環境中の日常物への反応が脳の異なる領域で制御されていると説明するため。
人々が顔を想像して見る日常的な物体の例を提供するため。
choiceAnalysis: 識別困難は述べていない
偽の顔が知覚される物体の具体例として列挙→正解。💡 本文の該当箇所と選択肢の表現を照合し、パラフレーズを見抜く
sourceEvidence: People perceive these so-called false faces on a variety of objects, from clouds and tree trunks to pieces of food and electric sockets

questions.question: Previous studies have shown that
questions.questionTranslation: 先行研究は何を示したか？
choices: people's judgments about what emotions real faces are expressing are influenced by other real faces they have seen immediately before.
people attach emotional meaning to false faces more quickly than they do to real faces.
people tend to judge the emotions expressed by false faces as happier and more positive than those expressed by real faces.
people take longer to distinguish false faces when the faces are not expressing any emotions.
choiceTranslations: 実際の顔が表す感情の判断は直前に見た他の実際の顔に影響される。
偽の顔に感情的意味を付与する方が実際の顔より速い。
偽の顔の感情をより幸せで肯定的だと判断する傾向がある。
感情を表していない偽の顔の区別に時間がかかる。
choiceAnalysis: 直前に見た顔が次の顔の感情判断に影響すると明記→正解。💡 本文中に明記された事実と選択肢の対応を確認。言い換え表現に注意
速度の比較は述べていない
より幸せとは述べていない
時間の比較は述べていない
sourceEvidence: people's judgment of what emotion a face is expressing is affected by the faces they have just seen

questions.question: What do the researchers in Australia say about the brain's ability to assess the emotions expressed by faces?
questions.questionTranslation: オーストラリアの研究者は顔の感情評価能力について何を述べているか？
choices: The ability will likely disappear over time as it no longer provides an advantage to humans in terms of survival.
The fact that the brain uses loose criteria to identify faces allows people to quickly judge the emotions faces express.
The brain is only able to accurately identify the emotions faces express if those faces have very specific features.
The evolution of this ability occurred even though it created disadvantages as well as benefits for humans in the past.
choiceTranslations: 生存の利点がなくなるため時間とともに消える可能性。
脳が緩い基準を使うことで表情を素早く判断できる。
非常に具体的な特徴がある場合のみ正確に識別できる。
過去に不利益と利益の両方を生んだにもかかわらず進化した。
choiceAnalysis: 一般的（緩い）基準→素早い評価と明記→正解。💡 本文中に明記された事実と選択肢の対応を確認。言い換え表現に注意
具体的ではなく一般的と明記
不利益との両立は研究者の主張ではない
sourceEvidence: the brain's criteria for recognizing a face are general rather than specific. This is one reason the brain can assess facial expressions so quickly

passages.label: B
passages.title: Durians and Giant Fruit Bats
paragraphs: The football-sized durian fruit is well known for its unpleasant smell and creamy, sweet flesh. Known as the "king of fruits," durians are believed to have originated in Borneo, but they are now cultivated more widely, with over half of all durians consumed worldwide being grown in Thailand. Durians have long been popular throughout Southeast Asia, but their popularity is now spreading to other parts of the world. There are hundreds of kinds of durians, but the Musang King variety, which is grown almost exclusively in Malaysia, is one of the most highly valued. Durians contain high levels of vitamins, so they are often promoted for their health benefits, which has led to rising exports. In fact, experts predict there will be a 50 percent increase in shipments from Malaysia to China alone during the next decade. In order to take advantage of this situation, many Malaysian farmers have stopped producing crops such as palm oil in favor of producing durians.

Durian trees are not easy to grow, however. They require regular watering and feeding with fertilizer, and they are highly sensitive to temperature. Furthermore, they do not naturally grow in groves, but rather thrive when grown among other trees and shrubs, so growing them in an orchard as a single crop presents a challenge. Ensuring sufficient pollination of the flowers for the trees to produce a good harvest of fruit is a further difficulty for farmers. One characteristic of durian trees is that their flowers only release pollen at night, so insects such as honeybees that feed during the day do not pollinate them. Animals that are active at night take over the role of pollination, but only about 25 percent of a durian tree's flowers ever get pollinated naturally. Because of this, many farmers resort to the labor-intensive practice of pollinating by hand.

Studies have shown that giant fruit bats are the main natural pollinators of durian flowers. However, these bats are chased away or killed by many farmers, who simply see them as pests because they cause damage and reduce profits by feeding on the fruit. The bats are also threatened as a result of being hunted and sold as food, since there is a belief in some Southeast Asian cultures that eating the bats' meat helps to cure breathing problems. Without educating people about the benefits of giant fruit bats, the bats' numbers may decline further, which could have serious consequences for durian farming.
translations: フットボール大のドリアンは不快な匂いとクリーミーで甘い果肉で知られる。「果物の王様」として知られ、ボルネオ島が原産だが今はより広く栽培されている。世界で消費されるドリアンの半分以上がタイで栽培される。東南アジアで長く人気だが、世界の他の地域にも広がっている。マレーシアのムサンキングは最も高く評価される品種の一つ。ビタミンが豊富で健康効果が宣伝されており輸出が増加している。マレーシアから中国への出荷だけで今後10年で50%の増加予測。多くのマレーシア農民がパーム油からドリアン生産に転換している。
しかしドリアンの木は栽培が簡単ではない。定期的な水やりと肥料が必要で温度に敏感。自然では林ではなく他の木や低木の中で育つため、果樹園で単一作物として栽培するのは困難。花の受粉を確保することも問題。ドリアンの花は夜にしか花粉を出さないため昼間のミツバチは受粉しない。夜行性の動物が受粉するが花の約25%しか自然に受粉しない。このため多くの農民が労力のかかる手作業での受粉を行う。
研究でオオフルーツコウモリがドリアンの花の主な自然受粉者であることが示されている。しかしこのコウモリは農民に害獣と見なされ追い払われたり殺されたりしている。コウモリ肉が呼吸の問題を治すという信仰もあり食用のために捕獲・販売されている。コウモリの利点について教育しなければ個体数がさらに減少し、ドリアン栽培に深刻な影響を及ぼす可能性がある。
sentencePairs: many Malaysian farmers have stopped producing palm oil in favor of durians
多くのマレーシア農民がパーム油からドリアンに転換
their flowers only release pollen at night
花は夜にしか花粉を出さない
only about 25 percent get pollinated naturally
約25%しか自然に受粉しない
giant fruit bats are the main natural pollinators
オオフルーツコウモリが主な自然受粉者
the bats' numbers may decline further
コウモリの個体数がさらに減少する可能性

questions.question: According to the first paragraph, what is true about durian production?
questions.questionTranslation: 第1段落によるとドリアン生産について正しいことは？
choices: Durians are now mainly grown in Malaysia because there is no longer enough land available to cultivate them in other Southeast Asian countries.
Although durians have been selling well in places where they were traditionally grown, they have yet to gain popularity in other countries.
Premium varieties of durians have been criticized by consumers because they have no more nutritional value than cheaper varieties.
Because of the increasing demand for durians, Malaysian farmers are switching from growing other crops to growing durians.
choiceTranslations: マレーシアが主な栽培地になった。
海外での人気はまだない。
プレミアム品種が栄養価で批判されている。
需要増によりマレーシア農民が他の作物からドリアンに転換している。
choiceAnalysis: 主にタイで栽培と述べている
世界に広がっていると明記
栄養価の批判は述べていない
パーム油からドリアンへの転換を明記→正解。💡 本文中に明記された事実と選択肢の対応を確認。言い換え表現に注意
sourceEvidence: many Malaysian farmers have stopped producing crops such as palm oil in favor of producing durians

questions.question: One factor that durian farmers need to consider is that
questions.questionTranslation: ドリアン農家が考慮すべき一つの要素は
choices: although durian trees can be grown in almost any warm climate, they do best in areas where there are few other plants growing.
the tendency of durian trees to push out other plants is causing a sharp decline in the number of native plants.
durian trees should be grown in a location where they can be easily found by honeybees and other daytime pollinators.
if durian trees are left alone to be pollinated naturally, the trees are unlikely to produce a large amount of fruit.
choiceTranslations: ほぼどんな温暖気候でも育てられるが、他の植物が少ない場所が最適。
他の植物を押し出す傾向が在来植物の減少を引き起こしている。
ミツバチなど昼間の受粉者に見つけてもらいやすい場所で育てるべき。
自然受粉に任せると大量の果実を生産しにくい。
choiceAnalysis: 他の木の中で育つ方が良い
押し出しは述べていない
ミツバチは受粉しない（夜に花粉を出す）
25%しか自然受粉しない→大量生産は困難→正解。💡 因果関係の問いでは、原因→結果の論理チェーンを正確に追跡する
sourceEvidence: only about 25 percent of a durian tree's flowers ever get pollinated naturally

questions.question: What is one thing the author of the passage says regarding giant fruit bats?
questions.questionTranslation: 著者がオオフルーツコウモリについて述べていることは？
choices: Durian production might suffer if awareness is not raised about the important role giant fruit bats play in durian flower pollination.
Many people in Southeast Asia have become ill as a result of eating bat meat that was sold illegally at some markets.
Some durian farmers deliberately attract giant fruit bats to their orchards so that they can catch them and sell their meat.
There has been a significant drop in natural pollinators of durian flowers because many giant fruit bats have died from breathing problems.
choiceTranslations: 受粉の重要な役割の認識が広がらなければドリアン生産が打撃を受ける。
コウモリの肉を食べて病気になった人がいる。
農民がコウモリを引きつけて肉を売っている。
呼吸の問題で多くのコウモリが死んだため自然受粉者が減少している。
choiceAnalysis: コウモリの減少がドリアン栽培に深刻な影響を及ぼす可能性を明記→正解。💡 本文中に明記された事実と選択肢の対応を確認。言い換え表現に注意
農民が引きつけるではなく追い払う
コウモリが病気で死んだとは述べていない
sourceEvidence: Without educating people about the benefits of giant fruit bats, the bats' numbers may decline further, which could have serious consequences for durian farming

passages.label: C
passages.title: The Long Range Desert Group
paragraphs: During World War II, the British fought against Germany and Italy in the deserts of North Africa. Desert warfare was characterized by small battles between troops that were widely spread out, and there was a need to move quickly and at night to avoid both detection and the dangerous daytime heat. The area's vast size and sandy terrain made transporting supplies difficult, and the lack of water severely limited operations. However, for one British army officer, Major Ralph Bagnold, these harsh conditions presented a strategic opportunity. Having spent years exploring the North African desert before the war, Bagnold knew the terrain well, and he was convinced that a small, highly mobile motorized unit that could observe and track enemy forces would be invaluable. At first, British commanders rejected his proposal to form such a unit, believing airplanes were better suited for such long-range intelligence gathering. Bagnold insisted, however, that gathering information on the ground would be advantageous, and his persistence led to the formation of the Long Range Desert Group (LRDG), with Bagnold as commander, in June 1940.

The LRDG was an unconventional unit from the outset. Usual distinctions between ranks did not apply; officers and regular soldiers were on first-name terms, and they were all expected to perform the same tasks. Rather than seeking men who would fight bravely on the battlefield, Bagnold wanted individuals with great stamina, resourcefulness, and mental toughness — men who could, for example, remain motivated and alert for extended periods despite limited access to drinking water. With specialized trucks adapted to desert conditions, the LRDG's patrols were equipped to operate independently for around three weeks and over a range of more than 1,600 kilometers. All necessary items, such as fuel, ammunition, and food, were carried by the unit, so careful supply planning was extremely important.

The LRDG's work mainly involved traveling deep behind enemy lines to observe their movements. The unit had access to a range of weaponry, and while the men were primarily trained to gather intelligence, they also planted mines and launched attacks against enemy airfields and fuel depots. When the Special Air Service (SAS) — a British army unit formed in 1941 to conduct raids behind enemy lines — suffered heavy casualties after parachuting into enemy territory on its first mission, the LRDG was tasked with bringing back the survivors. The rescue mission was a success, and because of its men's extensive knowledge of the desert, the LRDG was given the responsibility of bringing the SAS to and from all future targets by land, providing both transportation and navigation. This almost certainly helped the SAS accomplish its raids with greater success and fewer casualties.

The LRDG's greatest achievement came in 1943, when the unit found a route that enabled British forces to get around heavily defended enemy lines without being detected, allowing them to attack at weaker points in the defenses. This was a crucial turning point in the campaign in North Africa and contributed greatly to the British victory there. The LRDG went on to make significant contributions to the war effort in Europe until 1945.
translations: 第二次世界大戦中、イギリスは北アフリカの砂漠でドイツ、イタリアと戦った。砂漠戦は広く分散した部隊間の小規模戦闘が特徴で、検出と危険な日中の暑さを避けるため素早く夜間に移動する必要があった。広大な面積と砂地が補給輸送を困難にし、水不足が作戦を厳しく制限した。しかしラルフ・バグノルド少佐にとってこの過酷な条件は戦略的機会だった。戦前に砂漠探検の経験が豊富で地形を熟知しており、敵を観察・追跡できる小型の高機動部隊が極めて価値あるものになると確信していた。最初イギリスの指揮官は飛行機が情報収集に適していると考え提案を拒否したが、バグノルドの粘りで長距離砂漠グループ（LRDG）が1940年6月に結成された。
LRDGは最初から型破りな部隊だった。通常の階級の区別は適用されず、将校と一般兵はファーストネームで呼び合い全員が同じ任務を遂行した。戦場で勇敢に戦う人間ではなく、スタミナ、機知、精神的タフさを持つ個人を求めた。砂漠条件に適応した特殊トラックで約3週間、1,600km以上の範囲で独立運用できた。燃料・弾薬・食料のすべてを運搬したため慎重な補給計画が重要だった。

LRDGの任務は主に敵前線の奥深くに侵入して動きを観察することだった。情報収集が主任務だが地雷設置や敵飛行場・燃料貯蔵庫への攻撃も行った。SAS（1941年結成の英軍特殊部隊）が最初の任務で大きな損害を受けた際、LRDGが生存者の救出を任された。救出成功後、LRDGは砂漠の知識を活かしてSASの全将来目標への陸上輸送とナビゲーションを担当した。これによりSASはより成功し少ない損害で作戦を遂行できた。
LRDGの最大の功績は1943年で、堅固に守られた敵陣を迂回するルートを発見し、防御の弱い箇所から攻撃できるようにした。これが北アフリカ戦線の決定的転換点となりイギリスの勝利に大きく貢献した。LRDGは1945年まで欧州戦線でも重要な貢献を続けた。
sentencePairs: a small, highly mobile motorized unit would be invaluable
小型の高機動部隊が極めて価値ある
Usual distinctions between ranks did not apply
通常の階級の区別は適用されなかった
Bagnold wanted individuals with great stamina, resourcefulness, and mental toughness
スタミナ、機知、精神的タフさを持つ個人を求めた
the LRDG was given the responsibility of bringing the SAS to and from all future targets
LRDGがSASの全将来目標への輸送を担当
the unit found a route that enabled British forces to get around enemy lines
敵陣を迂回するルートを発見

questions.question: Major Ralph Bagnold was able to convince British army commanders that
questions.questionTranslation: ラルフ・バグノルド少佐はイギリス軍指揮官を何で説得できたか？
choices: their soldiers were having limited success on missions in the desert because they were not being supplied with the right resources.
the airplanes being used to fly over enemy territory and make observations in the desert were in need of major improvements.
he could lead a unit of men on missions in the desert despite the fact that he had little experience in such an environment.
using a ground-based unit to gather information about enemy activities in the desert would be an effective strategy.
choiceTranslations: 兵士が適切な資源を供給されていないため任務の成功が限られている。
偵察用飛行機に大幅な改善が必要。
砂漠経験が少ないにもかかわらず部隊を率いることができる。
地上部隊による敵情報収集が効果的な戦略になる。
choiceAnalysis: 資源の不足は述べていない
飛行機の改善は述べていない
経験豊富と述べている
地上での情報収集が有利と主張して説得→正解。💡 本文の該当箇所と選択肢の表現を照合し、パラフレーズを見抜く
sourceEvidence: Bagnold insisted that gathering information on the ground would be advantageous

questions.question: What is true regarding the Long Range Desert Group (LRDG)?
questions.questionTranslation: LRDGについて正しいことは？
choices: The characteristics of the men chosen for it and the way it operated were different from those of traditional military units.
Because of its limited budget, it had to manage with fewer resources and older weapons than other units.
There were a large number of men in its patrols, so the officers had to have special training in management techniques.
The success of its missions was heavily dependent on the group having supplies sent to it behind enemy lines on a regular basis.
choiceTranslations: 選ばれた人員の特徴と運営方法が従来の軍部隊とは異なっていた。
限られた予算のため他の部隊より少ない資源と古い武器で運営した。
パトロールに多数の人員がいたため将校は特別な管理訓練が必要だった。
任務の成功は敵前線後方への定期的な補給に大きく依存していた。
choiceAnalysis: 階級の区別なし、スタミナと機知を重視→従来と異なる→正解。💡 因果関係の問いでは、原因→結果の論理チェーンを正確に追跡する
予算の制限は述べていない
全物資を自ら運搬（補給は受けない）
sourceEvidence: The LRDG was an unconventional unit
Usual distinctions between ranks did not apply

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the relationship between the LRDG and the Special Air Service (SAS)?
questions.questionTranslation: LRDGとSASの関係を最もよく表しているのはどれか？
choices: The two units were combined so that land and air raids could be performed at the same time.
The similar nature of their operations led to competition between the two units and their unwillingness to assist each other.
The LRDG used its knowledge of the desert to help the SAS improve both the effectiveness and safety of its missions.
The involvement of the SAS in LRDG missions made it more difficult for the LRDG to stay behind enemy lines for long periods of time.
choiceTranslations: 陸上・航空作戦を同時に行うために統合された。
類似の任務が競争を生み互いへの支援を拒んだ。
LRDGが砂漠の知識を活かしてSASの任務の効果と安全性の向上を助けた。
SASの関与がLRDGの長期滞在を困難にした。
choiceAnalysis: LRDGの輸送とナビゲーションがSASの成功と損害減少に貢献→正解。💡 本文の該当箇所と選択肢の表現を照合し、パラフレーズを見抜く
sourceEvidence: This almost certainly helped the SAS accomplish its raids with greater success and fewer casualties

questions.question: According to the author of the passage, what happened in 1943?
questions.questionTranslation: 著者によると1943年に何が起きたか？
choices: A mistake made by the LRDG allowed enemy forces to strengthen their hold on territory that the British hoped to gain.
The transfer of the LRDG to Europe meant the SAS had no choice but to attack enemy forces in a heavily defended area without LRDG support.
The activities of the LRDG made it possible for the British army to gain a significant advantage that led to it defeating enemy forces in the area.
British commanders decided the LRDG would be better put to use defending British-held territory than observing enemy activities.
choiceTranslations: LRDGの失敗で敵が領土の支配を強化した。
LRDGの欧州移転でSASが支援なしで攻撃するしかなかった。
LRDGの活動でイギリス軍が大きな優位を得て敵を打ち破った。
LRDGを敵の観察より領土防衛に使うことが決まった。
choiceAnalysis: 迂回ルート発見が決定的転換点となりイギリスの勝利に貢献→正解。💡 本文の該当箇所と選択肢の表現を照合し、パラフレーズを見抜く
防衛へ転換は述べていない
sourceEvidence: This was a crucial turning point in the campaign in North Africa and contributed greatly to the British victory

focusPoints.title: 分詞構文と付帯状況のwith
focusPoints.subtitle: Participial Clauses & With-Constructions
focusPoints.explanation: 分詞構文に加え、with + O + 分詞/形容詞の独立分詞構文も頻出します。付帯状況（～しながら、～した状態で）を表す重要なパターンです。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: Having spent years exploring... / with Bagnold as commander

examples.en: Having spent years exploring the desert, Bagnold knew the terrain well.
examples.ja: 何年も砂漠を探検していたのでバグノルドは地形を熟知していた。
examples.note: 完了形分詞構文 = 主節より前の動作。

examples.en: With supplies running low, the team decided to return.
examples.ja: 補給品が少なくなってきたので、チームは帰還を決めた。
examples.note: with + O + -ing = 独立分詞構文。

examples.en: Based on its annual rate of growth, scientists estimate...
examples.ja: 年間成長率に基づき科学者は推定する。
examples.note: 過去分詞の分詞構文（受動・完了の意味）。

practiceQuestions.q: 'With the deadline approaching' は何構文？
practiceQuestions.a: 付帯状況のwith。with + 名詞 + 分詞/形容詞/前置詞句。「～が～している状態で」。
having reviewed = 完了分詞構文（主文より前の動作）。knowing = 同時進行の分詞構文（主文と同時）。

practiceQuestions.q: 'With all the data compiled' の compiled は？
practiceQuestions.a: 過去分詞。with + 名詞 + 過去分詞 = 「～が～された状態で」。受動の付帯状況。

practicePassage.en: With the deadline approaching, the team worked overtime to finish the report. The manager, having reviewed the draft, requested several revisions. The employees completed their tasks, knowing that the client expected the report by Friday. With all the data compiled, the final version was submitted ahead of schedule. Standing at the podium, the CEO presented the findings with confidence, impressing the board of directors.
practicePassage.ja: 締め切りが近づく中、チームは報告書を仕上げるために残業した。草稿を確認した後、マネージャーはいくつかの修正を要求した。クライアントが金曜日までに報告書を期待していることを知りながら、従業員はタスクを完了した。全データがまとめられた状態で、最終版は予定より早く提出された。壇上に立ち、CEOは自信を持って調査結果を発表し、取締役会を感銘させた。

focusPoints.title: 接続副詞と論理展開パターン
focusPoints.subtitle: Discourse Markers & Logical Development
focusPoints.explanation: Part 2の空所補充では文脈の論理的関係（補足、対比、具体例、因果）を示す接続表現の選択が求められます。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: In fact / In particular / However / Gradually

examples.en: In fact, heavy seasonal rainfall led to the formation of temporary lakes.
examples.ja: 実は、大量の季節的降雨が一時的な湖を形成した。
examples.note: in fact = 実は（前文の補足・強調）。

examples.en: In particular, the invention of the Ford Model T led to many new drivers.
examples.ja: 特に、フォード・モデルTの発明が多くの新しいドライバーを生んだ。
examples.note: in particular = 特に（具体例の導入）。

examples.en: Gradually, however, farmers began to change their minds.
examples.ja: しかし次第に農民は意見を変え始めた。
examples.note: however + gradually = 逆接＋段階的変化。

practiceQuestions.a: Accordingly = それに応じて（論理的帰結）。Subsequently = その後（時間的順序）。
譲歩の接続副詞。「確かに」と相手の主張を認めてから反論する。= It is true that...
同時に起きている別の出来事を紹介する。「一方、その間」。

practicePassage.en: The company had been struggling financially for several quarters. Accordingly, the board decided to implement a series of cost-cutting measures. Furthermore, they hired a new management team with expertise in corporate restructuring. Meanwhile, employees were asked to accept temporary salary reductions. Admittedly, the decisions were unpopular, but they proved to be effective. Subsequently, the company returned to profitability within two years.
practicePassage.ja: 会社は数四半期にわたり財政的に苦戦していた。それに応じて、取締役会は一連のコスト削減措置を実施することを決定した。さらに、企業再編の専門知識を持つ新しい経営陣を雇った。一方、従業員は一時的な給与削減を受け入れるよう求められた。確かに決定は不人気だったが、効果的であることが証明された。その後、会社は2年以内に黒字に戻った。

focusPoints.title: 関係代名詞と関係副詞の使い分け
focusPoints.subtitle: Relative Pronouns vs Relative Adverbs
focusPoints.explanation: who/which/that（関係代名詞）とwhere/when/why（関係副詞）の使い分け、および省略パターンを正確に理解しましょう。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: objects on which people imagine they can see faces / areas where...

examples.en: The area in which the fungus was found covers several acres.
examples.ja: 菌が発見された地域は数エーカーに及ぶ。
examples.note: 前置詞 + which = where の書き換え。

examples.en: The era when British rule began lasted almost a century.
examples.ja: イギリスの直接統治が始まった時代は約1世紀続いた。
examples.note: when = in which の書き換え。

examples.en: That is the reason why many species are declining.
examples.ja: それが多くの種が減少している理由だ。
examples.note: why = for which の書き換え。

practiceQuestions.a: where = in which。場所を表す関係副詞。the village where = the village in which。

practiceQuestions.q: 'for which he became famous' の前置詞は何に連動？
practiceQuestions.a: become famous for ～。前置詞+関係代名詞では、元の動詞句の前置詞が前に出る。

practiceQuestions.q: 'the meaning of which' はどんな用法？
practiceQuestions.a: 前置詞+関係代名詞の非制限用法。= and its meaning / whose meaning。

practicePassage.en: The village where the artist spent his childhood has become a popular tourist destination. The paintings for which he became famous depict scenes from his early life. The period in which he was most productive coincided with a time of great personal loss. His most celebrated work, the meaning of which has been debated for decades, hangs in a London gallery. Few people are aware of the circumstances under which his final masterpiece was created.
practicePassage.ja: その芸術家が幼少期を過ごした村は人気の観光地になった。彼が有名になった絵画は幼少期の風景を描いている。彼が最も生産的だった時期は大きな個人的喪失の時期と一致した。彼の最も有名な作品はその意味が何十年も議論されており、ロンドンのギャラリーに飾られている。彼の最後の傑作が作られた状況を知っている人はほとんどいない。

focusPoints.title: so/such...that構文と程度・結果表現
focusPoints.subtitle: So/Such...That & Degree-Result Patterns
focusPoints.explanation: so＋形容詞/副詞＋that、such＋名詞＋that は程度と結果を表す重要構文です。too...to と enough to の使い分けも含みます。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: so quickly / such a challenge that...

examples.en: The problem was so complex that no one could solve it alone.
examples.ja: 問題があまりにも複雑で誰も一人では解決できなかった。
examples.note: so + 形容詞 + that = あまりに～なので。

examples.en: It was such a nuisance that they left it covered.
examples.ja: あまりにも厄介だったのでカバーしたままにした。
examples.note: such + 名詞 + that = あまりに～な（名詞）なので。

examples.en: The hotel is not tall enough to obstruct the view.
examples.ja: ホテルは景色を遮るほど高くない。

practiceQuestions.q: 'so powerful that' と 'such magnitude that' の違いは？
practiceQuestions.a: so + 形容詞 + that。such + 名詞 + that。so = 形容詞/副詞を修飾。such = 名詞（句）を修飾。

practiceQuestions.q: 'Such was the force' と 'So extensive was the destruction' は何？
practiceQuestions.a: 倒置のso/such...that構文。強調のために主語と動詞が入れ替わる文語的表現。

practiceQuestions.q: 'of such magnitude' を so に書き換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: The damage was so great/severe that it took several years to rebuild.

practicePassage.en: The earthquake was so powerful that buildings across the city collapsed within seconds. Such was the force of the tremor that rescue teams from neighboring countries were immediately dispatched. The damage was of such magnitude that it took several years to rebuild the affected areas. So extensive was the destruction that the government declared a national emergency. The loss of life was such that international mourning was observed for a week.
practicePassage.ja: 地震は非常に強力で市全体の建物が数秒で崩壊した。揺れの力はあまりにも大きく、近隣国からの救助隊がただちに派遣された。被害はあまりにも大規模だったため、被災地の再建には数年かかった。破壊があまりにも広範だったため、政府は国家非常事態を宣言した。人命の損失はあまりにも大きく、1週間の国際的な追悼が行われた。

focusPoints.title: 今回の重要なパラフレーズ
focusPoints.subtitle: Key Paraphrases in This Exam
focusPoints.explanation: 選択肢の正答は本文表現の言い換えです。同じ意味を異なる言葉で表現する力が合否を分けます。
focusPoints.sourceQuote:
①thaw = defrost
②instill = implant/cultivate
③ridiculed = mocked
④notorious = infamous
⑤cover up = conceal

examples.en: had a surprising origin → started by cyclists, not car drivers
examples.ja: 意外な起源→自動車ではなく自転車乗りが起源
examples.note: 予想を裏切るパラフレーズ。

examples.en: developed a sophisticated society → wells, organized homes, religion
examples.ja: 洗練された社会→井戸、整然とした住居、宗教

examples.en: unconventional unit → no rank distinctions, different selection criteria
examples.ja: 型破りな部隊→階級なし、異なる選抜基準
examples.note: 複数の要素で一語を言い換え。

practiceQuestions.q: 'undergoing a fundamental transformation' を言い換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: experiencing a major overhaul / going through a radical change / being fundamentally reshaped

practiceQuestions.q: 'compelled to adapt their production methods' を言い換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: forced to change how they manufacture / obligated to modify their manufacturing processes

practiceQuestions.q: 'attracted substantial investment' を言い換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: secured significant funding / drawn considerable financial backing / received large amounts of capital
becoming increasingly popular / growing in appeal / rising in demand

practicePassage.en: The automotive industry is undergoing a fundamental transformation driven by advances in electric and autonomous vehicle technology. Traditional manufacturers are being compelled to adapt their production methods to remain competitive. Startups specializing in electric vehicles have attracted substantial investment from venture capitalists. Consumer attitudes toward car ownership are also shifting, with ride-sharing services gaining popularity in urban areas. Analysts predict that within the next decade, the majority of new cars sold will be electric.
practicePassage.ja: 自動車産業は電気自動車と自律走行車技術の進歩によって根本的な変革を遂げている。従来のメーカーは競争力を維持するために生産方法を適応させることを余儀なくされている。電気自動車を専門とするスタートアップはベンチャーキャピタリストから多額の投資を集めている。自動車所有に対する消費者の態度も変化しており、都市部ではライドシェアサービスの人気が高まっている。アナリストは今後10年以内に販売される新車の大半が電気自動車になると予測している。
